description: Fix two confirmed permission bugs (resetStaleReviewedVersions not clearing per-tool overrides, SearchResults missing browserPermission props) and add comprehensive E2E test coverage for browser tool permission flows, Restore approvals button, and config.json verification.

userStories.title: Clear per-tool overrides in resetStaleReviewedVersions on plugin version update
userStories.description: resetStaleReviewedVersions resets permission to 'off' and clears reviewedVersion when a plugin updates, but does not clear the tools map. Per-tool overrides survive the version reset and take precedence in getConfiguredToolPermission, bypassing the re-review requirement.
acceptanceCriteria: resetStaleReviewedVersions sets config.tools to undefined when resetting a stale reviewedVersion
After a plugin version update, getConfiguredToolPermission returns 'off' for all tools (no surviving per-tool overrides)
Unit test verifies per-tool overrides are cleared on version mismatch
Unit test verifies config.json reflects cleared tools after version reset
userStories.notes:
Files:
- platform/mcp-server/src/reload.ts (resetStaleReviewedVersions function)
- platform/mcp-server/src/reload.test.ts (reviewedVersion reset describe block)

Context:
`resetStaleReviewedVersions` (reload.ts:198-223) iterates `state.pluginPermissions` and resets plugins whose `reviewedVersion` doesn't match the installed version. Currently it sets `config.permission = 'off'` and `config.reviewedVersion = undefined` but does NOT clear `config.tools`.

The bug: `getConfiguredToolPermission` (state.ts:383) resolves as `pluginConfig.tools?.[toolName] ?? pluginConfig.permission ?? 'off'`. If a tool has `tools: { send_message: 'auto' }` and the plugin updates, the per-tool override survives and the tool resolves to 'auto' despite the plugin being reset to 'off'.

Changes:
In `resetStaleReviewedVersions` (reload.ts:212-213), add `delete config.tools;` (or `config.tools = undefined;`) after `config.reviewedVersion = undefined;`:
```
config.permission = 'off';
config.reviewedVersion = undefined;
// ADD THIS LINE:
delete config.tools;
```

Note: `config` is typed as `PluginPermissionConfig` which has `tools?: Record<string, ToolPermission>`. Using `delete config.tools` or assigning `undefined` both work — use `delete` to match the pattern used in handleConfigSetPluginPermission.

For tests, add to the 'reviewedVersion reset on plugin update' describe block in reload.test.ts:
1. A test with `permissions: { 'my-plugin': { permission: 'auto', tools: { test_tool: 'auto' }, reviewedVersion: '0.9.0' } }` that verifies after reload both `permission` is 'off' and `tools` is undefined.
2. A test verifying the persisted config.json also has no `tools` key after version reset.

The existing test 'version mismatch resets permission to off and clears reviewedVersion' (reload.test.ts:634) can serve as a template — it already uses `createPluginDir` and `writeFileSync` to set up the scenario.

Constraints:
- Do NOT change handleConfigSetPluginPermission — it already clears tools via destructuring (`const { tools: _cleared, ...rest } = pConfig`).
- Do NOT change getConfiguredToolPermission or getToolPermission.
- The 'browser' pseudo-plugin skip at line 202 must remain.

userStories.title: Pass browserPermission and onBrowserPermissionChange to BrowserToolsCard in SearchResults
userStories.description: SearchResults renders BrowserToolsCard without passing browserPermission or onBrowserPermissionChange. This causes the browser plugin-level select to show 'Off' during search regardless of actual state, and plugin-level permission changes are silently dropped because the callback is undefined.
acceptanceCriteria: SearchResultsProps includes browserPermission and onBrowserPermissionChange fields
SearchResults forwards browserPermission and onBrowserPermissionChange to BrowserToolsCard
App.tsx passes browserPermission and setBrowserPermission to SearchResults
Browser plugin-level select in search results reflects the actual configured permission
userStories.notes:
Files:
- platform/browser-extension/src/side-panel/components/SearchResults.tsx (add props, forward to BrowserToolsCard)
- platform/browser-extension/src/side-panel/App.tsx (pass new props to SearchResults)

Context:
App.tsx renders SearchResults at line 405 without browserPermission or setBrowserPermission. In the non-search path (line 428), BrowserToolsCard receives both props correctly. SearchResults.tsx renders BrowserToolsCard at line 91-97 without these props, causing BrowserToolsCard to default browserPermission to 'off' and onBrowserPermissionChange to undefined.

Changes:
1. In SearchResults.tsx, add to SearchResultsProps interface (after line 16):
```
browserPermission: ToolPermission;
onBrowserPermissionChange: (permission: ToolPermission) => void;
```
Import ToolPermission: `import type { ToolPermission } from '@opentabs-dev/shared';`

2. In the SearchResults component destructuring (line 40-58), add the new props.

3. In the BrowserToolsCard JSX (lines 91-97), add:
```
browserPermission={browserPermission}
onBrowserPermissionChange={onBrowserPermissionChange}
```

4. In App.tsx (lines 405-423), add to the SearchResults JSX:
```
browserPermission={browserPermission}
onBrowserPermissionChange={setBrowserPermission}
```

Constraints:
- Do NOT change BrowserToolsCard.tsx — it already handles these props correctly with defaults.
- The ToolPermission type import in SearchResults.tsx must use the type-only form to avoid CSP issues (see CLAUDE.md 'Side Panel Import Constraints').

userStories.title: E2E test: browser plugin-level permission change via side panel
userStories.description: Add an E2E test that changes the browser card's plugin-level permission via the side panel PermissionSelect and verifies all individual tool descriptions update in MCP tools/list and config.json is persisted correctly.
acceptanceCriteria: E2E test changes browser plugin-level permission from 'auto' to 'off' via the side panel
Test verifies all browser tools gain '[Disabled]' prefix in MCP tools/list
E2E test changes browser plugin-level permission back to 'auto'
Test verifies all browser tools lose '[Disabled]' prefix
Test verifies config.json reflects the permission change
userStories.notes:
Files:
- e2e/side-panel-plugin-toggle.e2e.ts (add new test)

Context:
The existing 'plugin permission select changes the default for all tools' test (line 402) only covers the e2e-test plugin, NOT browser tools. Browser tools use a different card component (BrowserToolsCard) with a different handler path (setPluginPermission('browser', ...)).

Changes:
Add a new test.describe block 'Side panel — browser tool plugin-level permission' with a test that:
1. Creates a config with `browser: { permission: 'auto' }` and the e2e-test plugin with `permission: 'auto', reviewedVersion: pluginVersion`
2. Starts MCP server with `OPENTABS_DANGEROUSLY_SKIP_PERMISSIONS: ''`
3. Opens side panel, verifies browser card visible
4. Uses `selectPermission(sidePanelPage, 'Permission for browser plugin', 'Off')` to change browser to off
5. Verifies via `mcpClient.listTools()` that browser tools have '[Disabled]' prefix
6. Uses `readTestConfig(configDir)` to verify `config.permissions.browser.permission === 'off'`
7. Changes back to 'auto' and verifies tools work again

The aria-label for the browser plugin-level select is 'Permission for browser plugin' (see BrowserToolsCard.tsx:168).

Use the existing `selectPermission` helper from helpers.ts. Use the `BROWSER_TOOL_NAMES` constant from helpers.ts to check a subset of browser tools.

Template: follow the pattern of the 'plugin permission select changes the default for all tools' test at line 402 — same fixture setup pattern with `fs.mkdtempSync`, `writeTestConfig`, `startMcpServer`, `launchExtensionContext`, `setupAdapterSymlink`.

Constraints:
- Use `OPENTABS_DANGEROUSLY_SKIP_PERMISSIONS: ''` to disable skipPermissions so selects are interactive.
- Do NOT test individual browser tool permission changes in this story — that's a separate story.

userStories.title: E2E test: individual browser tool permission change via side panel
userStories.description: Add an E2E test that changes an individual browser tool's permission via the side panel and verifies the change is reflected in MCP tools/list and config.json.
acceptanceCriteria: E2E test expands the browser card and changes one tool from 'auto' to 'off'
Test verifies the specific tool gains '[Disabled]' prefix in MCP tools/list while others remain enabled
Test verifies config.json has a per-tool override for the disabled tool
E2E test changes the tool back to 'auto' (matching plugin default) and verifies the per-tool override is removed from config.json (smart cleanup)
userStories.notes:
Files:
- e2e/side-panel-plugin-toggle.e2e.ts (add new test)

Context:
No E2E test exercises individual browser tool permission changes. BrowserToolsCard.tsx:91-103 handles this via `handleToolPermissionChange` which calls `setToolPermission('browser', toolName, permission)`. The server handler `handleConfigSetToolPermission` has smart cleanup that removes per-tool overrides matching the plugin default.

Changes:
Add a test in a new describe block 'Side panel — individual browser tool permission change' that:
1. Creates config with `browser: { permission: 'auto' }` and e2e-test plugin with `permission: 'auto', reviewedVersion: pluginVersion`
2. Starts MCP server with `OPENTABS_DANGEROUSLY_SKIP_PERMISSIONS: ''`
3. Opens side panel, expands browser card by clicking accordion trigger
4. Uses `selectPermission(sidePanelPage, 'Permission for browser_list_tabs tool', 'Off')` to disable one tool
5. Verifies via `mcpClient.listTools()` that `browser_list_tabs` has '[Disabled]' prefix but other browser tools don't
6. Reads `readTestConfig(configDir)` and verifies `config.permissions.browser.tools.browser_list_tabs === 'off'`
7. Changes the tool back to 'auto' via selectPermission
8. Verifies the per-tool override is removed from config (smart cleanup): `config.permissions.browser.tools` should be undefined or not contain `browser_list_tabs`

The aria-label for individual browser tools follows the pattern `Permission for <tool_name> tool` (see ToolRow.tsx:100).

To expand the browser card, click the accordion trigger: `sidePanelPage.locator('button[aria-expanded]').filter({ hasText: 'Browser' }).click()`

Constraints:
- Pick `browser_list_tabs` as the test tool — it's a simple read-only tool unlikely to cause side effects.
- Browser tools card does NOT have an unreviewed dialog, so no special handling needed.

userStories.title: E2E test: Restore approvals button click persists across permission changes
userStories.description: Add an E2E test that clicks the 'Restore approvals' button and verifies skipPermissions stays off even after subsequent permission changes trigger hot reloads.
acceptanceCriteria: E2E test starts with skipPermissions enabled (banner visible)
Test clicks 'Restore approvals' and verifies the banner disappears
Test changes a plugin permission and verifies the banner does NOT reappear
Test verifies permission selects remain interactive after restoring approvals
userStories.notes:
Files:
- e2e/side-panel-plugin-toggle.e2e.ts (add new test, ideally in the existing 'skipPermissions mode and group headers' describe block)

Context:
The existing skipPermissions test (line 531) verifies the banner appears and selects are interactive, but does NOT test the 'Restore approvals' button. The button calls `bridgeSetSkipPermissions(false)` (App.tsx:361). A prior bug caused the banner to reappear after permission changes because `performReload` re-read the env var — this was fixed (reload.ts now preserves `state.skipPermissions`), but no E2E test guards against regression.

Changes:
Add a new test in the 'skipPermissions mode and group headers' describe block (or a sibling describe) that:
1. Creates config with e2e-test plugin at 'auto' (reviewed) and browser at 'auto'
2. Starts MCP server WITH `OPENTABS_DANGEROUSLY_SKIP_PERMISSIONS: '1'` (skipPermissions enabled)
3. Opens side panel, verifies the 'Approvals skipped' banner is visible
4. Clicks 'Restore approvals' button: `sidePanelPage.getByRole('button', { name: 'Restore approvals' }).click()`
5. Verifies the banner disappears: `await expect(sidePanelPage.getByText('Approvals skipped')).toBeHidden({ timeout: 5_000 })`
6. Changes browser permission to 'off' via selectPermission
7. Waits briefly (1-2 seconds) for any plugins.changed to propagate
8. Verifies the banner is still hidden (skipPermissions wasn't re-enabled)
9. Optionally: changes the permission back and verifies selects still work

Constraints:
- The banner text is 'Approvals skipped' (App.tsx:351).
- The button text is 'Restore approvals' (App.tsx:359-362).
- This test MUST use `OPENTABS_DANGEROUSLY_SKIP_PERMISSIONS: '1'` (not '') to enable the banner.
- After restoring, permission changes trigger hot reload in dev mode. The test must verify skipPermissions survives this reload.
- Mark this as e2eCheckpoint: true since it's the final story that touches browser behavior.
